Northern Pacific railway and to the
International Encyclopedia, published
by Dodd, Mead, & Co.


In 1902 Spokane, by actual tabula-
ton. got 1900 visitors. This year
no committee being appointed for
that purpose. no count has been kept,
but it is conservative to say that
fully twe-thirds of last years num-
Der came into the Inland Empire
chamber of commerce.


The chamber of commerce has also
played a Legislative part. making ef-
forts to open the south half OF the
Colyille Indian reservation; starting
the good roads movement and the
formation Of a county good roads as
sociation: securing appropriations for
opening UP the upper Columbia river
for navigation: assisting in the mat
ter Of increasing the facilities ut
Fort Wright SO as to make 1t a regi-
mental post and the construction of
a steel bridge across the Spokane
river to directly connect the city with
the post; aiding in building an ar
m0ry building. and bringing to Spo-
kane the proposed Norwegian college.


Much more can be written of the
efforts to locate mills and factories
here. To do these things there have
been held since the first of the year
II board meetings, two general meet
ings and four special meetings, be-
sides numerous meetings OF the diff.
ferent committees.


Added to It's other functions. the
chamber Of commerce has entertain.
ed several distinguished individuals
and parties visiting the city. Sena
tors Ankeny and Foster, General Fun.
ston and Major Andrews were enter
tanned. as well as the North Dakott
| Editorial association and the Penn
sylvania and New York delegates t
the national Presbyterian assembl,
at Los Angeles.
